name: Marshmallows
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Gelatin soaked in 1/2 cup Cold Water
recipeInstructions:
Marshmallows from Marge Osborn's 9th grade Home Ec Class. They can also be used as centers for dipped chocolates or bark.
1. Mix sugar and 3/4 cup water in a heavy-bottomed pan. Cook to soft ball stage, 238 degrees.
2. Remove from heat and stir in gelatin until dissolved.
3. Pour mixture into a glass mixing bowl. Allow to cool slightly, then whip until thick and white. Add vanilla extract.
4. Mix 2 tablespoons powdered sugar and 2 tablespoons cornstarch and spread into a 9-inch square pan. Pour candy into the prepared pan, let stand. Cut into squares using a wet knife and give a final dusting of powdered sugar.
